New to WIC
New to WIC
En Español
WIC office
_____________
WIC is here for you!
If you are pregnant, recently had a baby, or have a child under 5, WIC is here to support you & your family.
Complete the
Welcome to WIC
class and survey before your first appointment. You can choose to complete your lesson in English or Spanish
Get started:
1. Visit
wichealth.org
2. Click the "
Sign Up
" button
3. Create an account and fill in the information. Remember to:
Type in your 9-digit
Family ID number
Select the WIC Agency
County of San Luis Obispo
Select your
WIC location
4. Create a profile
5. Click "
Begin
" to start or continue a lesson
6. Find a Lesson - select
"I prefer to browse on my own"
7. Choose
Welcome to WIC
(first tile) WIC Orientation class for all new and returning applicants - complete the lesson and survey before your first appointment

What do I bring to my first WIC appointment?
1. Each person who will be enrolling into the WIC program and
identification
for yourself and any children under age 5
2. A document verifying your
current address,
such as a paycheck with your address or utility bill. If you don't have an address, let your WIC counselor know
3. A document verifying your
current income,
such as
pay stubs or cash receipts before taxes for the past 30 days for all family members working in the household or active CenCal/Medi-Cal
4.
Proof of pregnancy
- if applicable
Download the California WIC App today!
-
Check your food balance, scan your WIC foods and more on your phone
iPhone
I
Android
FAQs
▾
How do I start a lesson?
1. Log in to your
wichealth.org
2. Click "Start Lesson"
3. Choose a lesson
4. Complete the lesson
5. Fill out the survey
6. Get WIC class credit
▾
How do I update my profile?
You will need to update your current profile with your 9-digit Family ID number. Follow these steps:
1. Log in to your
wichealth.org
account
2. Click on "Edit Profile" on the right side of the screen
3. Check "Your WIC Information"
4. Select "Show/Update Family ID"
5. Enter your new 9-digit Family ID number
6. Click "Update"
▾
What is "Welcome to WIC"?
When you sign up for wichealth.org, you can complete the Welcome to WIC lesson which is required for new and returning applicants to the program. It's a great way to learn how you can make the most of your WIC benefits.
▾
Where can I get help?
To get help with
wichealth.org
, here are several options:
On
wichealth.org
:
Click on "Trouble Signing In?" located below the "Log In" and "Sign Up" buttons and follow the steps
Click on the orange "Help" or question mark button on the bottom of the screen and search your question
Call or text our County of San Luis Obispo WIC Program:
phone: (805) 781-5570 or text: (888) 417-6180 (Monday - Friday 8:00-5:00 PM)
▾
WIC Office Locations and Hours
Find a
WIC office
nearest you.
▾
How do I activate my WIC card?
To activate your new WIC Card and set a 4-digit PIN, call Customer Service at 1-844-469-3264 (back of the WIC card).
You will need to know:
The 16-digit WIC Card number
Your date of birth (two digits for the month and day and four digits for the year)
Your mailing address ZIP code
Choose a 4-digit pin that is easy for you to remember and difficult for others to guess
You will need the 4-digit pin to use your WIC Card at the store
